metadata.title: Stellar/XLM, Jed McCaleb, and the 'tacostand' XRP sell-down — the Ripple fork lineage and the settlement wallet (documented, dated)
metadata.status: the Ripple->Stellar fork, the SDF founding, the 2014 McCaleb settlement terms, the 'tacostand' wallet, and Stellar's institutional partners are FACT (Wikipedia, Ripple/SDF posts, on-ledger data, partner PRs); extends existing McCaleb/Stellar/SDF/Circle/DTCC/Franklin Templeton nodes rather than duplicating them
metadata.frame: Sub-block of the Ripple/XRPL investigation (#171). The McCaleb lineage and the famous 'tacostand' saga. Jed McCaleb co-founded Ripple, left (2013-14) over decentralization disagreements, and co-founded the Stellar Development Foundation (SDF) with Joyce Kim — Stellar initially forked the Ripple codebase, then rewrote consensus as SCP (2015). His ~9B XRP was put under a 2014 settlement that capped daily selling; the on-ledger 'tacostand' wallet he sold from was finally emptied in Jul 2022. Stellar's DOCUMENTED institutional ties (Circle/USDC, DTCC, Franklin Templeton, MoneyGram) are real and already in the graph. Extends Jed_McCaleb, Stellar, Stellar_Development_Foundation, XRP, Ripple, MtGox, Circle, DTCC, Franklin_Templeton.

the_lineage: Jed McCaleb (earlier: founder of Mt. Gox in 2010, sold to Karpelès 2011; and eDonkey) co-founded Ripple (OpenCoin) and was CTO until ~2013; he left amid disputes over Ripple's direction/decentralization and his role. In 2014 he co-founded the non-profit Stellar Development Foundation (SDF) with Joyce Kim. Stellar initially FORKED the Ripple codebase, then McCaleb + Graydon Hoare rewrote the consensus mechanism as the Stellar Consensus Protocol (SCP, 2015) — making XLM/Stellar a technically distinct, more-nonprofit-governed cousin of XRP/XRPL. grade: fact.
the_settlement: McCaleb received ~9B XRP at the XRPL's 2012 creation. After his exit, a 2014 settlement with Ripple constrained his selling to avoid crashing the market: his (and his children's) remaining ~5.3B XRP went into escrow with release tied to a percentage of daily XRP trading volume; he agreed to DONATE 2B XRP to a charitable donor-advised fund and to sell all his Ripple equity. The agreement was renegotiated over the years. grade: fact.
the_tacostand: The 'tacostand' is the community/on-ledger nickname for the XRPL account McCaleb sold his settlement XRP from (tracked publicly on XRPL explorers for years as a market-overhang gauge). He sold the LAST of it in July 2022 — Ripple itself marked the moment with a post ('The Stand is Finally Out of Tacos'). So 'Jed's Tacostand' is a real, documented on-ledger wallet, not a metaphor — and a clean example of how XRPL transparency let the community track an insider's sell-down in real time. grade: fact.
the_partners: Stellar's DOCUMENTED institutional footprint (already in the graph, reaffirmed here): Circle issues USDC natively on Stellar; the DTCC has run tokenization work touching Stellar; Franklin Templeton's on-chain US Government Money Fund (FOBXX/BENJI) runs on Stellar; and MoneyGram built a USDC-on-Stellar remittance/cash-in-cash-out partnership (2021-22). IBM's earlier 'World Wire' cross-border product was built on Stellar (later wound down). grade: fact (partner PRs); 'wound-down vs active' varies by product.
the_honest_reading: The McCaleb/Stellar story is well-documented and needs no embellishment: a co-founder split (2013-14) produced a technically-distinct, nonprofit-governed cousin chain (Stellar/XLM via SCP), and a court-style settlement governed his ~9B XRP — visible to anyone on-ledger as the 'tacostand' wallet until it emptied in Jul 2022. Stellar's real institutional ties (Circle, DTCC, Franklin Templeton, MoneyGram) are concrete and already mapped. The value here for #171 is the clean, on-chain-verifiable contrast to the unverifiable XRP lore: where the ledger is public (McCaleb's sales), the facts are crisp; where claims rest on 'hidden' integrations, they are not. Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: Wikipedia — Jed McCaleb (Mt. Gox, Ripple exit, Stellar/SDF with Joyce Kim)
Ripple — 'The Stand is Finally Out of Tacos' (McCaleb's settlement wallet emptied, 2022)
The Daily Hodl — Ripple settlement wallet drained as McCaleb settlement nears completion (2021)

edges.raw_instrument: co-founded Ripple (OpenCoin), CTO until ~2013; departed 2013-14 over decentralization/direction disputes
co-founded the Stellar Development Foundation with McCaleb (2014)
sold his settlement XRP from the on-ledger 'tacostand' XRPL account
McCaleb's ~9B XRP sold under the 2014 Ripple settlement (escrow + daily-volume cap; 2B donated to charity); emptied Jul 2022 ('The Stand is Out of Tacos') — a publicly-trackable insider sell-down
